mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, creating negative real rates that incentivize equity risk-taking and compress discount rates on future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis zero-rate environment with persistent inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of periods when both FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll growth (PAYEMS) decelerates below 100k new jobs added, signaling weakening labor demand that triggers equity market repricing downward as investors anticipate slower earnings growth and reduced consumer spending capacity over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100k jobs added in the prior month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% quarter-over-quarter, indicating a contraction in residential investment demand that signals broader economic weakness and reduces forward equity returns as construction and related industries face margin pressure over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% from prior quarter average
regime_hint: demand-constrained environment with housing market weakness
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 12-month trend by more than 1.5 percentage points, indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and drive positive equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth rate exceeds 12-month average by > 1.5pp
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with improving capacity utilization
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline simultaneously by > 5% month-over-month, signaling synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand that forces equity repricing downward as forward earnings expectations compress over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 5% from prior month
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month window while payroll growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates, indicating deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity market repricing as investors anticipate reduced consumer spending and earnings headwinds over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS monthly change < 100k
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment and slowing job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 0.5% while core inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, creating a regime of negative real rates that persists despite inflation pressure, signaling central bank accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) contract month-over-month, indicating demand-side weakness that compresses forward earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND RSAFS declines > 3% from prior month
regime_hint: demand-constrained late-cycle environment with slowing growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 63% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
